summary: The diff shows components wired to React Query mutations (like/unlike, add comment, follow/unfollow, delete post) and introduces optimistic behavior for at least profile save and post create inside `frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts`. However, the provided `useQueries.ts` diff is truncated before showing optimistic implementations for several required mutations (likes, comment likes, follow/unfollow, delete post), so full REQ-6/7/8 coverage cannot be verified from the available evidence.

missing_requirements.requirement: Implement consistent optimistic UI updates for all key mutations (post like/unlike, comment add, comment like/unlike, follow/unfollow, post delete, etc.) by moving optimistic behavior into React Query mutation hooks in `frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts` using `onMutate`/`onError` rollback and only targeted invalidation when needed.
missing_requirements.reason: The diff snippet of `useQueries.ts` only clearly shows `onMutate`/rollback for saving the caller profile and part of optimistic post creation. The required optimistic mutation implementations for post like/unlike, comment add, comment like/unlike, follow/unfollow, and post delete are not visible in the truncated diff, so their presence cannot be confirmed from evidence provided.
evidence: frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts (truncated; only `useSaveCallerUserProfile` and start of `useCreatePost` optimistic logic shown)
frontend/src/components/PostCard.tsx (calls like/unlike mutations but does not show cache update logic)
frontend/src/components/CommentsModal.tsx (calls addComment/likeComment/unlikeComment mutations but does not show cache update logic)
frontend/src/pages/CreatorProfilePage.tsx (calls follow/unfollow/delete mutations but does not show cache update logic)

missing_requirements.requirement: Update UI components to rely on unified optimistic React Query cache state rather than per-component local optimistic state, ensuring identical optimistic behavior across entry points (PostCard vs PostDetailModal; CommentsModal vs PostDetailModal; follow/unfollow on profiles).
missing_requirements.reason: Components still derive `isLiked`/counts from the `post` prop (e.g., `post.likes`) rather than reading a cached post entity keyed by query state; the diff does not show that `post` props are sourced from the same unified cache-updated query results in all entry points. Also, per-component UI-only states (e.g., like animation/success flags) remain, and the diff is truncated so we cannot verify that prior local optimistic state was removed everywhere it should be.
evidence: frontend/src/components/PostCard.tsx (computes `isLiked` from `post.likes` on the passed-in `post` prop)
frontend/src/components/CommentsModal.tsx (uses local `showSuccess` and local input state; cache reliance for optimistic comment list cannot be confirmed due to truncation)

missing_requirements.requirement: Make post create/delete feel instant by applying optimistic cache updates to the feed (`['feed']`) and user posts (`['userPosts', principal]`) lists with rollback on error.
missing_requirements.reason: Optimistic create behavior for `['feed']` and `['userPosts', authorPrincipal]` is visible. However, optimistic delete behavior removing from both lists (and rolling back on error) is not shown in `useQueries.ts` due to truncation; only components calling `useDeletePost()` are shown.
evidence: frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts (shows optimistic insertion into `['feed']` and `['userPosts', authorPrincipal.toString()]` for create; delete not visible due to truncation)
frontend/src/components/PostDetailModal.tsx (invokes `useDeletePost` but no cache update logic shown here)
frontend/src/pages/ProfilePage.tsx (invokes `useDeletePost` but no cache update logic shown here)

hallucinated_features.description: Documentation claims broad UI/theme/branding changes (e.g., 'iOS 26-inspired OKLCH color system', new logo/favicon, new Inbox page, MainLayout navigation changes, EditProfileModal upload progress tracking, enhanced image preloading), but these changes are not reflected in the actual modified file list shown in the diff summary.
hallucinated_features.reason: The only evidence for these items is text inside `frontend-file-summaries.txt`, while the actual diff summary lists modifications to only 9 files and does not include `frontend/src/index.css`, `frontend/index.html`, `frontend/src/pages/InboxPage.tsx`, `frontend/src/components/MainLayout.tsx`, or `frontend/src/hooks/useImagePreloader.ts` as modified files.
evidence: frontend-file-summaries.txt (contains claims about many files/features not present in the modified-files list)
File diff summary modified-files list (only 9 files; does not include the files claimed as changed in `frontend-file-summaries.txt`)
